faq.question: What is the golden ratio?
faq.answer: The golden ratio (φ ≈ 1.618) is a mathematical proportion found in nature and art. Two quantities are in the golden ratio when their ratio equals the ratio of their sum to the larger quantity.

faq.question: How do I use the golden ratio in photography?
faq.answer: Place key subjects at golden ratio points (about 38.2% and 61.8% of the frame). This creates more dynamic compositions than centering, similar to but more refined than the rule of thirds.

faq.question: Is the golden ratio better than the rule of thirds?
faq.answer: They're similar — the rule of thirds divides at 33%/67%, while the golden ratio divides at 38.2%/61.8%. The golden ratio places subjects slightly closer to center, which many find more pleasing.

faq.question: Is the Golden Ratio Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard photography formulas trusted by photographers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Golden Ratio Calculator?
sections.body: The Golden Ratio Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your known value, calculate, you get instant results including longer segment, shorter segment, ratio check. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting longer segment right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?

It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
